James' Morning Briefing

Monday, April 6, 2026
Top Story

Israel attacks major Iranian petrochemical site after Trump's infrastructure threats

Israel claims the site produces half of Iran's petrochemical output. The Israeli attack follows Donald Trump threatening more US attacks if the Strait of Hormuz is not open by Tuesday.

Read Full Story →

World News

Israel attacks major Iranian petrochemical site after Trump's infrastructure threats

Israel claims the site produces half of Iran's petrochemical output. The Israeli attack follows Donald Trump threatening more US attacks if the Strait of Hormuz is not open by Tuesday.

BBC · Middle East · Read more

US carries out daring rescue of service member in Iran

A mountain hideout and aircraft under fire: The operation to extract the airman from hostile territory was hugely complex and involved multiple US government agencies.

AP · Middle East · Read more

Trump issues expletive-laden threat to Iran over Hormuz Strait blockage

The US president says he will destroy Iranian power plants and bridges if the vital waterway is not reopened before an apparent deadline.

BBC · Middle East · Read more

IAEA confirms military strikes close to Iran's Bushehr nuclear power plant

The International Atomic Energy Agency confirmed recent impacts of military strikes but said the plant itself was not damaged.

Community supporting family after body was pulled from Green Lake.

Technology

Foxconn reports 29.7% revenue rise on strong AI demand

Taiwan's Foxconn, the world's largest contract electronics maker, saw first-quarter revenue surge on strong demand for artificial intelligence products.

Reuters · News · Read more

NASA's Perseverance rover completes first AI-planned Mars drives

Using Anthropic's Claude vision-language models, the rover analyzes orbital imagery and terrain data to autonomously generate safe waypoints.

Crescendo AI · News · Read more

AI companies shatter fundraising records with $297B in Q1

OpenAI, Anthropic, Waymo and other AI companies hauled in $297 billion in funding in the first three months of 2026.

New development from Google could address one of the most significant limitations currently facing AI systems.
